title: The Salt Procession
description: With the tree's fate decided, the parish's survivors walk the old salt-road one last time — and they need wardens.

scenes.description: A ragged column of survivors follows the drowned causeway, scattering grave-salt as they go. The marsh watches from both sides.

choices.label: Walk point ahead of the column
choices.resultText: You take the front. Whatever comes for the procession meets you first.

choices.label: Guard the stragglers
choices.resultText: You fall back with the old and the slow. The column tightens.

scenes.description: The procession shelters in the half-sunk chapel while the tide of the marsh turns. Mother Brine, the salt-keeper, watches you re-draw her lines with narrowed eyes.

choices.label: Hold the doors until dawn
choices.resultText: All night, things test the salt. All night, the lines hold. By morning, Mother Brine nods at you — once.

choices.label: Lead a quiet prayer
choices.resultText: The words are old and the roof leaks, but for an hour the parish is a parish again. Mother Brine mouths the names with you.

choices.label: Demand her grave-salt stores
choices.resultText: Mother Brine hands over the sack without a word. The survivors move a little further from you.

scenes.description: At the causeway's end, dry land — and morning. The survivors count each other, then look back once at the Hollows.

choices.label: See them to the hills
choices.resultText: You walk the parish out of its grave. What they build next is theirs; that they build at all is yours.

choices.label: Accept Mother Brine's blessing
choices.resultText: She presses a pinch of grave-salt into each of your palms. 'The dead will know you kept faith,' she says. 'So will I.'
